deal_flags.reason: target legal counsel identified from first Background-section meeting attendance
deal_flags.source_quote: By invitation of the Board, Stewart G. MacDonald, Jr., our chief executive officer, Michael J. Shea, our executive vice president and chief financial officer, Neil MacLellan and Phil Emma, executive vice presidents at Mac-Gray, Linda Serafini, our vice president, general counsel and secretary, and representatives from BofA Merrill Lynch and Goodwin Procter LLP, outside legal counsel to Mac-Gray, were also in attendance for all or portions of the meeting.

deal_flags.reason: acquirer legal counsel identified from merger-agreement negotiations
deal_flags.source_quote: On September 25, 2013, Goodwin Procter delivered to Kirkland & Ellis LLP, outside legal counsel to CSC/Pamplona, whom we refer to as Kirkland, the initial draft of the merger agreement.

events.additional_note: BofA Merrill Lynch engagement for the 2011-2012 Discussions.
events.source_quote: Certain potential strategic transactions have been discussed from time to time between Mac-Gray and CSC and other participants in the laundry facilities management industry, including discussions between Mac-Gray and CSC in the winter of 2011 and into 2012 regarding the possible acquisition of CSC by Mac-Gray. In connection with Mac-Gray's consideration of a possible acquisition of CSC, which we refer to as the 2011-2012 Discussions, Mac-Gray engaged BofA Merrill Lynch on October 23, 2012. During the fall and winter of 2012, CSC engaged in a sale process and, ultimately, CSC was purchased by Pamplona in May 2013.

events.additional_note: Board authorized management to explore transactional opportunities.
events.source_quote: As part of its ongoing review of strategic alternatives, at an in-person meeting of the Board on April 5, 2013, the Board discussed the sale of CSC to Pamplona as well as industry-wide merger and acquisition activity and dynamics, and authorized Mac-Gray management to work with BofA Merrill Lynch to explore transactional opportunities that might be available to Mac-Gray, including opportunities with another strategic party, whom we refer to as Party A, in the laundry facilities management industry. The Board also determined to further consider Mac-Gray's strategic alternatives at its meeting scheduled for May 9, 2013.

events.additional_note: Target-side outreach to Party A before Party A made a proposal.
events.source_quote: On April 8, 2013, representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch, as instructed by the Board, telephoned a representative of Party A to discuss generally a possible business combination between Party A and Mac-Gray. During this call, the representative of Party A indicated that Party A might consider a business combination with Mac-Gray.
On May 15, 2013, Mac-Gray sent a letter to BofA Merrill Lynch terminating their engagement in connection with the 2011-2012 Discussions.

events.additional_note: New engagement for review of strategic alternatives and potential sale process.
events.source_quote: On May 31, 2013, Mac-Gray entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch with respect to the Board's undertaking a review of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale process, and in which BofA Merrill Lynch agreed not to engage as a possible source of new acquisition financing for any potential buyers in connection with any such sale process without Mac-Gray's prior written consent.

events.process_conditions_note: Unsolicited proposal before Party A executed a confidentiality agreement.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: pre-NDA unsolicited range proposal and preliminary sale-process context.
events.source_quote: On June 21, 2013, Party A submitted an unsolicited proposal to the Mac-Gray Board of Directors offering to purchase Mac-Gray for an all-cash purchase price of $17.00 to $19.00 per share, which we refer to as the Party A June 21 proposal.

flags.reason: Party A submitted $17.00-$19.00/share proposal on 2013-06-21; NDA executed on 2013-08-05.
range bid: $17.00 to $19.00 per share

events.additional_note: Special Committee determined to conduct a sale process and approach a bidder universe.
events.source_quote: In light of the factors discussed at this meeting, the Special Committee concluded that it would be in the best interests of stockholders to consider a potential sale of Mac-Gray and to review this alternative as compared to Mac-Gray's continuing as a stand-alone company and the risks and opportunities associated with execution of Mac-Gray's strategic business plan.

Later on June 28, 2013, Party B, entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Mac-Gray and was provided with an informational package for the purpose of providing Party B with data to formulate a preliminary indication of interest.

On June 30, 2013, Party C entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Mac-Gray and was provided with an informational package for the purpose of providing Party C with data to formulate a preliminary indication of interest.

On July 11, 2013, CSC/Pamplona entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Mac-Gray and were provided with an informational package for the purpose of providing CSC/Pamplona with data to formulate a preliminary indication of interest.

flags.reason: Filing narrates CSC/Pamplona confidentiality agreement as one group event; bidder_alias=CSC/Pamplona.

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: preliminary indication of interest in initial round.
events.source_quote: On July 23, 2013, CSC/Pamplona submitted a preliminary indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $18.50 per share.

During the next several weeks, in accordance with the directives of the Special Committee, BofA Merrill Lynch contacted the strategic and financial parties identified at the June 24th Special Committee meeting. Over the next two months a total of 20 potential bidders, including two strategic bidders (Party A and CSC/Pamplona) and 18 financial bidders (including Party B and Party C), entered into confidentiality agreements with Mac-Gray on a form provided by Mac-Gray, the terms of which were individually negotiated with potential bidders, but did not vary materially from the form provided.

flags.reason: phrase: 'Over the next two months'; midpoint from 2013-06-24 anchor: 2013-07-24
16 unnamed financial-bidder NDA rows complete the stated 18 financial bidders after Party B and Party C.

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: preliminary indication of interest in initial round.
events.source_quote: On July 24, 2013, Party B submitted a preliminary indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $17.00 to $18.00 per share.

flags.reason: range bid: $17.00 to $18.00 per share

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: oral preliminary indication of interest in initial round.
events.source_quote: Also on July 24, 2013, representatives from Party C called BofA Merrill Lynch and presented an oral preliminary indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $15.00 to $17.00 per share.

flags.reason: range bid: $15.00 to $17.00 per share

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: written indication of interest in initial round.
events.additional_note: Written indication following prior oral indication.
events.source_quote: Later on July 25, 2013, Party C submitted a written indication of interest at an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $16.50 per share.

flags.reason: range bid: $16.00 to $16.50 per share

events.source_quote: Discussions with Party A resumed on July 27, 2103 and Party A and the Transaction Committee agreed to the terms of the customer and employee non-solicitation provisions. On August 5, 2013, Party A entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Mac-Gray and was provided with an informational package for the purpose of providing Party A with data to potentially improve the terms of the Party A June 21 proposal.

On August 27, 2013, BofA Merrill Lynch, as instructed by the Special Committee, sent a letter to each of Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona instructing such bidders to submit revised written proposals no later than September 9, 2013 if such bidders wished to proceed further in the process to pursue an in-depth evaluation of Mac-Gray with the objective of making a revised offer.

flags.reason: no explicit final-round phrase; inferred from subset process letter requesting revised proposals

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: revised indication of interest, not yet definitive agreement stage.
events.source_quote: On September 9, 2013, CSC/Pamplona submitted a revised indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $19.50 per share, indicating that Pamplona was committed to provide 100% of the capital required to consummate the transaction and requesting that CSC/Pamplona and Mac-Gray agree to exclusive negotiations for two weeks.

events.process_conditions_note: Proposed combination of affiliated-fund equity and third-party debt; no firm financing commitment.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: revised indication with no firm financing commitment.
events.source_quote: On September 9, 2013, Party B also submitted a revised indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.50 per share and proposed to finance the transaction with a combination of equity from affiliated funds and third party debt. Party B's revised indication of interest did not include a firm financing commitment.

events.additional_note: Deadline for revised indications requested on August 27.
events.source_quote: On August 27, 2013, BofA Merrill Lynch, as instructed by the Special Committee, sent a letter to each of Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona instructing such bidders to submit revised written proposals no later than September 9, 2013 if such bidders wished to proceed further in the process to pursue an in-depth evaluation of Mac-Gray with the objective of making a revised offer.

flags.reason: no explicit final-round phrase; inferred from subset process letter and revised-indication deadline

events.process_conditions_note: Outstanding credit facilities or affiliated equity sources.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: revised indication of interest, no firm financing commitment.
events.source_quote: On September 10, 2013, Party A submitted a revised indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.00 to $19.00 per share, to be financed by outstanding credit facilities or affiliated equity sources.

flags.reason: range bid: $18.00 to $19.00 per share

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: oral revised indication with no firm financing commitment.
events.source_quote: Also on September 10, 2013, in a telephone call to a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch, a representative of Party C indicated that it was submitting a revised oral indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share. Neither Party A's nor Party C's revised indication of interest included a firm financing commitment.

flags.reason: range bid: $16.00 to $17.00 per share

events.additional_note: Request for final indications of interest by September 18.
events.source_quote: Based on the amount of due diligence conducted by Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona, and discussions with such parties to date, and in an effort to maximize value for Mac-Gray's stockholders, the Special Committee instructed BofA Merrill Lynch to call each of Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona to request final indications of interest by September 18, 2013 and to indicate that Mac-Gray would consider engaging in a short period of exclusive negotiations with one of the bidders depending upon, among other things, the proposed purchase price and the level of deal certainty indicated in the bidder's final proposal.

flags.reason: no explicit final-round phrase; inferred from request for final indications from four bidders

events.process_conditions_note: Pamplona committed to provide 100% of capital; requested two-week exclusivity.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1: submitted after final-indications request with 100% committed capital.
events.additional_note: Proposal requested exclusive negotiations for two weeks.
events.source_quote: On September 18, 2013, CSC/Pamplona submitted a proposal for an all-cash purchase price of $20.75 per share, reiterating that Pamplona was committed to provide 100% of the capital required to consummate the transaction and requesting that CSC/Pamplona and Mac-Gray agree to engage in exclusive negotiations for two weeks.

events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1: response to final-indications request and expressly best and final offer.
events.additional_note: Party A reiterated prior range as best and final offer.
events.source_quote: Also on September 18, 2013, Party A reiterated in a telephone call to a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch that its previous indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.00 to $19.00 per share was its best and final offer.

flags.reason: range bid: $18.00 to $19.00 per share
Range value coexists with formal trigger phrase 'best and final offer'.

events.process_conditions_note: Cash plus options representing 10% of new equity; options vest on achievement of base case management projections.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1: final-indications request; proposal stated total value and financing structure.
events.additional_note: Incremental $2.50/share value was Party B options, representing 10% of new equity with a strike price equal to Party B's initial cost basis.
events.source_quote: On September 18, 2013, Party B also submitted a revised indication of interest with a purchase price that Party B valued at $21.50 per share, including $19.00 of cash to be paid at closing and the remaining per share price to be paid in the form of options to be issued to Mac-Gray stockholders, representing 10% of the new equity with a strike price equal to Party B's initial cost basis. These options, which we refer to as the Party B options, would vest on the achievement of the base case management projections ( *i.e.* , over five years), excluding any future acquisitions. Party B's proposal stated that it valued the Party B options at $2.50 of incremental value per share.

flags.reason: Party B options treated as earnout-like contingent value because vesting depended on projections.

events.additional_note: Party C did not submit or reiterate a final indication.
events.comments: Bidder self-withdrew by not submitting a final indication; no reasons specified.
events.source_quote: Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest of an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share nor did it specify any reasons in connection therewith.

events.additional_note: Deadline for final indications of interest.
events.source_quote: Based on the amount of due diligence conducted by Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona, and discussions with such parties to date, and in an effort to maximize value for Mac-Gray's stockholders, the Special Committee instructed BofA Merrill Lynch to call each of Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona to request final indications of interest by September 18, 2013 and to indicate that Mac-Gray would consider engaging in a short period of exclusive negotiations with one of the bidders depending upon, among other things, the proposed purchase price and the level of deal certainty indicated in the bidder's final proposal.

flags.reason: no explicit final-round phrase; deadline for final indications of interest

events.additional_note: Party A was not selected after lower best-and-final range.
events.source_quote: Following a discussion, the Special Committee concluded that the revised indication of interest submitted by CSC/Pamplona had the potential of delivering the most value to stockholders, provided greater certainty and involved less potential risk than the other indications.

events.additional_note: Special Committee rejected Party B despite facially higher value because of contingent options and financing risk.
events.comments: Target dropped the highest facial value bid due to contingent/deferred option value and financing risk.
events.source_quote: The Special Committee discussed the risks and complexities of the Party B options, including their contingent and deferred nature and the uncertainties associated with how Mac-Gray's performance would be measured, especially in light of the fact that current Mac-Gray management would not be in control of the surviving corporation to monitor or drive the growth necessary to trigger payment under the Party B options.

events.process_conditions_note: Last and best offer conditioned on two-week exclusive negotiations and MacDonald voting agreements.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1: last and best offer after final round, with no financing contingency.
events.source_quote: On September 21, 2013, the members of the Transaction Committee called the representative of CSC/Pamplona to propose that Mac-Gray was prepared to move forward with CSC/Pamplona on an exclusive basis for two weeks subject to CSC/Pamplona increasing their all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share. Later that same day, the representative of CSC/Pamplona called the members of the Transaction Committee to confirm that CSC/Pamplona were willing to increase their all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share as a last and best offer, provided that Mac-Gray agree to a two week period of exclusive negotiations.

events.process_conditions_note: Merger agreement executed; post-execution announcement folded into this row.
events.additional_note: Agreement and Plan of Merger executed; announcement occurred before market open the following day.
source_quote: Later in the day, on October 14, 2013, the merger agreement was executed, the Pamplona commitment letter was delivered, Moab and Parent executed the Moab voting agreement and the Mac-Donald voting agreements became effective.
On October 15, 2013, before the U.S. stock markets opened, Mac-Gray and CSC jointly announced the execution of the merger agreement.
